description: Satoshi Nakamoto mines the first block. Mining is performed using standard CPUs on personal computers, representing the ultimate period of hobbyist decentralization.

Public release of GPU mining software. Miners discover that Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) are significantly more efficient at parallel hash calculations than CPUs, marking the first major hardware leap.
The first mining pool, Slush Pool, is launched. It introduces the concept of pooled mining to reduce payout variance for individual participants as network difficulty begins to rise.
The brief FPGA (Field-Programmable Gate Array) era begins. These chips offer better energy efficiency than GPUs but serve primarily as a bridge to specialized hardware.
The original Stratum protocol is introduced to replace 'getwork,' improving communication efficiency between miners and pools and becoming the industry standard for the next decade.
Canaan Creative ships the first ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit) miners. This permanently moves mining from general-purpose home hardware to specialized industrial machines.
GHash.io briefly controls over 50% of the network hashrate, sparking intense community debate over pool centralization and leading to a voluntary reduction in their power.
Following the 2016 halving, Bitmain-affiliated pools (AntPool, BTC.com) begin to dominate. This era is defined by the rapid scaling of high-efficiency ASIC chips and pool consolidation.
China's ban on mining triggers a massive global shift in hashrate. Miners migrate to North America and Kazakhstan, significantly increasing the market share of Western pools.
Foundry USA becomes the first North American pool to take the #1 global spot, signaling the entry of institutional capital and public companies into the mining sector.

Mining operations increasingly focus on sustainable energy and grid balancing. Reports highlight that over 50% of the network is now powered by sustainable sources as climate concerns influence policy.
Block rewards drop to 3.125 BTC. The network sees a renewed push for Stratum V2 adoption to allow miners to select their own transactions and decentralize pool power.
Leading pools like Foundry expand into multi-asset, institutional-grade services, offering compliance-first infrastructure for a wide range of decentralized protocols.
